title: Lane 2 forest sub-ecosystem content
summary: Deepen forest old-growth, under-root, canopy, and cave-adjacent teaching content without changing traversal or station systems.

execution_order.goal: Scope a forest sub-ecosystem content pack that builds on old-growth and under-root discoveries.
execution_order.why_now: Forest now has richer physical spaces that need educational fuel.

execution_order.goal: Implement the forest content pack through existing content-facing seams.
execution_order.why_now: Canopy and cave-adjacent spaces should teach structure, moisture, shelter, and decay.

execution_order.goal: Review forest content for science accuracy, compactness, and no traversal drift.
execution_order.why_now: Content should support exploration without becoming geometry work.

completion_gate: Forest sub-ecosystem teaching is richer on existing content surfaces.
No geometry, station, route-board, save, or traversal framework changes land.
Focused content tests and build pass.
guardrails: Do not add another cave system.
Favor habitat structure and decomposition over fauna sprawl.
Keep claims broad, source-safe, and ledger-backed.
post_packet_path: If clean, proceed to high-country source content.
If a spatial need appears, hand it to lane 3.

scout_491_handoff.selected_scope: A close-look-only payoff for the existing `western-hemlock-seedling` entry, reinforcing old-wood nursery and canopy microhabitat teaching without new content surfaces.
implementation_contract: Use callouts like `tiny needles` and `nurse wood` plus one compact source-safe sentence about damp old wood as a seedbed.

Do not edit forest entries, sprites, placements, ecosystem notes, observation prompts, station surfaces, route behavior, save state, geometry, traversal, page shells, rewards, or broad `game.ts` scope.
Do not add a `fallen-giant-log` close-look in this pass.

implemented_scope: Added `western-hemlock-seedling` close-look support with compact old-wood nursery copy.
Updated focused close-look allowlist and payload assertions.

critic_491_review.review_summary: The hemlock seedling close-look payoff is source-safe, compact, and lane-local. It reuses an existing verified entry and sprite, adds only focused close-look support and assertions, and avoids content-surface, station, route, save, geometry, traversal, page, reward, or `game.ts` drift.
